citations.quote: Fwd: You have a notification from SAAS

findings.text: Anfal received an 'Accommodation Arrears - Urgent attention required' email from Kathleen Donnelly/fees@napier.ac.uk on 8 Apr 2026.
Anfal received an 'Accommodation Hardship Fund - payment date' notification from findafund@napier.ac.uk on 26 Nov 2025.
A tenancy agreement (NAP-SRC-0018) and a student licence (attached to NAP-SRC-0028) exist but full terms are not extracted.
No payment ledger, invoice, formal demand, or arrears amount is present in the provided source materials.

proposed_artifacts.content:
## Bundle 12 Evidence Gap Report

### Arrears-Specific Sources Found
- **NAP-SRC-0021**: 'Accommodation Arrears - Urgent attention required' email (8 Apr 2026) – truncated, no amount or breakdown.
- **NAP-SRC-0022**: 'Accommodation Hardship Fund - payment date' (26 Nov 2025) – suggests prior hardship application.

### Missing Critical Evidence
- Payment ledger or account statement showing arrears balance and payment history.
- Formal demand letter or notice of intended legal proceedings.
- Full text of the arrears email (NAP-SRC-0021) including any attached breakdown.
- Tenancy agreement (NAP-SRC-0018) and student licence (NAP-SRC-0028) – full extraction needed for financial clauses.
- Any correspondence from the university's fees team beyond the single email.

### Recommended Next Steps
1. Extract full text of NAP-SRC-0021 (arrears email) to capture amount, due date, and any attached documents.
2. Request payment ledger from the university or student records.
3. Fully extract tenancy agreement (NAP-SRC-0018) and student licence (NAP-SRC-0028) for rent amount, payment terms, and late payment clauses.
4. Search for any formal demand or notice of intended legal action.
proposed_artifacts.title: Bundle 12 Evidence Gap Report: Arrears-Specific Sources

proposed_tasks.instructions: Extract the complete text of source NAP-SRC-0021 ('Accommodation Arrears - Urgent attention required') including the email body, any attachments, and metadata. Capture the arrears amount, due date, and any payment instructions or warnings. Output as a structured extraction artifact.
proposed_tasks.title: Extract full text of arrears email NAP-SRC-0021

redaction_flags.text: NAP-SRC-0034 contains a forwarded personal email (albshryanfal4@gmail.com) which may include third-party or private financial data.

risk_flags.text: Missing financial records (ledger, invoices) prevent verification of the claimed arrears amount and timeline.
No formal demand or notice of intended legal action found; the urgency of the arrears email is unsubstantiated.
Hardship fund notification (NAP-SRC-0022) and SAAS notification (NAP-SRC-0034) suggest financial vulnerability but no evidence of resolution.

summary: Bundle 12 evidence gap analysis: identified one direct arrears source (NAP-SRC-0021, 8 Apr 2026) and one hardship fund notification (NAP-SRC-0022, 26 Nov 2025). No payment ledger, invoice, or formal demand found. Tenancy agreement (NAP-SRC-0018) and licence (NAP-SRC-0028) present but not fully extracted. Proposed follow-up task to extract full arrears email and locate financial records.

uncertainties.text: The exact arrears amount, due date, and any prior payment history are not available from the current source materials.
The full text of the arrears email (NAP-SRC-0021) is truncated; the body and any attached breakdown are missing.
The tenancy agreement (NAP-SRC-0018) and student licence (NAP-SRC-0028) are only partially extracted; key financial clauses may be missing.
